business_ideas.idea: Premium meat supply business targeting high-end restaurants with custom blends.
Defense contractor R&D focused on cost-reduction vs cost-plus model.
Award show recognition for accountants and CPAs nationwide.
Scout and mentor 'hacker kids' (11-19) doing low-status passionate projects.

predictions.text: Homeschooled kids with contrarian thinking become world-changing founders.

disagreements: Sam and Shaan debate whether life outcomes are predetermined by circumstance vs personal responsibility.
dynamics: Hosts discuss deep business strategy and founder psychology through historical narratives. Shaan drives ideation; Sam pushes on first principles. Analytical, brainstorming tone focused on how winners identify market inefficiencies.
frameworks: Idiot Index - measure R&D investment % as signal of company health
Sensitivity + Audacity + Logic - three traits of world-changing founders
Don't sell commodities, create brands and lock in customers with exclusivity
Cost-plus model incentivizes waste vs product-first pricing drives efficiency
learnings: Best businesses own a small niche completely rather than broad markets
Founder psychology matters: willingness to rebel against parent/mentor wisdom at right moments
Counter-intuitive decisions during crises separate winners from survivors
Homeschooling and outcast status may create founder mentality
Government contracting has perverse incentives that startups can exploit

banger_quotes.quote: You can't hide your sins in a hamburger - quality shows.
100% revenue into R&D every year creates durable advantage.
The idiot tax is everything we don't question deeply.

numbers_dropped: $28 Black Label burger at Minetta Tavern sold 15,000+ units
1994 Pat Jr. takes over with 44 customers, 5 employees
Anduril investing 100% revenue vs competitors 1% on R&D
Amazon convinced investors of 20-year profit reinvestment
notable: Core thesis: best founders combine counter-intuitive insight + audacious execution + sensitivity to inefficiency; homeschooling linked to founder mindset.
